3.why: There is no value to build a sequence from.
3.invariant: An empty array has no run.

3.happening: The array has values, so we proceed.
3.why: We will sort them to find runs.

4.why: An empty array has no consecutive sequence.

5.why: Consecutive values then sit right next to each other.

6.happening: Start the current run length at 1.
6.why: Any single value is a run of one.
6.invariant: current_count tracks the run ending at i.

7.happening: Start the best run length at 1.
7.why: The answer is at least 1 for a non-empty array.
7.invariant: max_count is the best run so far.

8.happening: Scan from the second element with index i.
8.why: Each value is compared to its left neighbor.
8.invariant: Values left of i are already scanned.

9.happening: Is nums[{i}] the same as the previous value?
9.why: Duplicates must not extend or reset a run.

10.why: It does not change the run.

11.happening: Is nums[{i}] exactly one more than the previous value?
11.why: That is what makes the run consecutive.
11.invariant: A run grows only on a +1 step.

12.why: This value continues the consecutive sequence.
12.invariant: current_count counts the run ending here.

14.happening: A gap broke the run â€” reset to 1.
14.why: This value starts a fresh run.

15.happening: Record the best run length so far.
15.why: We keep the longest run seen.

16.invariant: The answer is the longest run.

init.happening: Sort the array and set up counters.

check.happening: Compare this value to its neighbor.

update.happening: Grow or reset the run and track the best.
update.why: Maintain the current and best run lengths.
update.invariant: Counters reflect the run ending at i.
